title: Hospet Church
tagline: Hospet Church — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: The Church of Holy Cross of Hospet (Portuguese: Igreja da Santa Cruz Hospet) in Dakshina Kannada, is one of the oldest churches in the Mangalore Diocese of India.
overview.description: The Church of Holy Cross of Hospet (Portuguese: Igreja da Santa Cruz Hospet) in Dakshina Kannada, is one of the oldest churches in the Mangalore Diocese of India. Being situated in Hosabettu, it is also known as Hospet Church. This was the only church that escaped the drive for the demolition of churches by Tipu Sultan during the Captivity of Mangalorean Catholics at Seringapatam.

topPlaces.name: Hosabettu
topPlaces.description: Hosabettu is a village located in Mangalore Taluk of Dakshina Kannada District in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located near Moodbidri and Mangalore.

topPlaces.name: Yedapadavu
topPlaces.description: Yedapadavu is a former village (township) in Mangalore taluk, Karnataka, India, that was divided into Badagayedapadavu (North Yedapadavu) and Tenkayedapadavu (South Yedapadavu). Yedapadavu now gives its name, as "Yedapadav" to the gram panchayat for those two villages.

topPlaces.name: Tenkayedapadavu
topPlaces.description: Tenkayedapadavu is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively, it is under Mangalore taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Kuppepadavu
topPlaces.description: Kuppepdavu, is a small town in Karnataka, India, 27 km away from Mangalore city and 340 km away from its State Capital City Bangalore. Bantwal is 15 km away, Moodbidri is 16 km away and Bajpe is 12 km away.

topPlaces.name: Mijar
topPlaces.description: Mijar is a village in the Manglore taluk in the city of Mangaluru of Dakshina Kannada district of the Indian state of Karnataka. Tenkamijaru Grama Panchayat which is 30 km from Mangalore, 12 km from the famous pilgrimage site Jain Kashi.

topPlaces.name: St. Patrick Church, Siddakatte
topPlaces.description: The St. Patrick Church is a Roman Catholic Church situated in the locality of Siddakatte, Bantwal.

topPlaces.name: Kotibasadi
topPlaces.description: Koti Basadi (also known as Koti Shetty Basadi) is a Jain temple located in Moodabidri, Karnataka, India. It is one of the 18 major Jain temples in the region, known for its distinct architecture and historical significance associated with the merchant community of the 15th century.

topPlaces.name: Guru Basadi
topPlaces.description: Guru Basadi is a basadi or Jain temple located in Moodabidri town in the Indian state of Karnataka. The Guru basadi is the oldest amongst 18 Jain basadis in Moodabidri built in 714 CE. This temple is near another Jain temple, Saavira Kambada Basadi.

items.note: Hosabettu is a village located in Mangalore Taluk of Dakshina Kannada District in the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located near Moodbidri and Mangalore.

Yedapadavu is a former village (township) in Mangalore taluk, Karnataka, India, that was divided into Badagayedapadavu (North Yedapadavu) and Tenkayedapadavu (South Yedapadavu). Yedapadavu now gives its name, as "Yedapadav" to the gram panchayat for those two villages.
Tenkayedapadavu is a panchayat village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. Administratively, it is under Mangalore taluk of Dakshina Kannada district in Karnataka.

Kuppepdavu, is a small town in Karnataka, India, 27 km away from Mangalore city and 340 km away from its State Capital City Bangalore. Bantwal is 15 km away, Moodbidri is 16 km away and Bajpe is 12 km away.

Mijar is a village in the Manglore taluk in the city of Mangaluru of Dakshina Kannada district of the Indian state of Karnataka. Tenkamijaru Grama Panchayat which is 30 km from Mangalore, 12 km from the famous pilgrimage site Jain Kashi.
The St. Patrick Church is a Roman Catholic Church situated in the locality of Siddakatte, Bantwal.

Koti Basadi (also known as Koti Shetty Basadi) is a Jain temple located in Moodabidri, Karnataka, India. It is one of the 18 major Jain temples in the region, known for its distinct architecture and historical significance associated with the merchant community of the 15th century.

Guru Basadi is a basadi or Jain temple located in Moodabidri town in the Indian state of Karnataka. The Guru basadi is the oldest amongst 18 Jain basadis in Moodabidri built in 714 CE. This temple is near another Jain temple, Saavira Kambada Basadi.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hospet Church?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hospet Church is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hospet Church?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (13 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hospet Church?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hospet Church at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hospet Church?
faq.a: Stays in Hospet Church start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hospet Church?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hosabettu, Yedapadavu, Tenkayedapadavu, Kuppepadavu, Mijar.
